Notes: Lubinski+2010 bright hard-X-ray state. Main compPS slab value uses geom=1; geom=-1 is a seed-photon-distribution systematic only. Optical depth is the full compPS slab depth; y calculations should not include an extra factor of 2.
Lubinski+2010 dim hard-X-ray state. Physically distinct from the bright state and kept as a separate compPS entry. Optical depth is the full compPS slab depth; no extra factor of 2 in y.

Brenneman+2014 NuSTAR-only slab compPS result. Same 2012 epoch as the compTT/S24 result, so keep only as a separate model-family comparison, not as an independent observation in a combined statistic. Eddington ratio is the epoch-literature value; S24/BASS gives a lower homogeneous catalog value.

Balokovic+2015 2013 science NuSTAR observation; adopted old-epoch compPS main value. The 2012 calibration observation is similar but omitted from the source-level main CSV. High slab optical depth is near or beyond the comfortable compPS slab regime; keep caveat. Classified as a Seyfert 1.9 in the source-specific NuSTAR/XMM studies; S24 lists it more generically, so the type column is standardized to Sy1.9.

Serafinelli+2023 2022 May+November XMM/NuSTAR+NuSTAR tied COMPPS result; adopted as the 2022 compPS main value instead of Marinucci+2022 because it includes two 2022 epochs and has smaller uncertainties. Eddington ratio is approximate, scaled from the 2022 L2-10 luminosity using the Balokovic+2015 bolometric-correction scale. Low y under the full-depth compPS convention should be treated as model-dependent. Classified as a Seyfert 1.9 in the source-specific NuSTAR/XMM studies; S24 lists it more generically, so the type column is standardized to Sy1.9.
